channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: SIDE-HUSTLES: There are not only Crypto and Stocks to make money — 24 replies — 96.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
SIDE-HUSTLES: There are not only Crypto and Stocks to make money

Open thread • Replies: 24 • Updated: 2026-04-23 16:41 UTC

OP

Ladies and Gents,

This market sucks. The governments (nearly worldwide) suck. Here in Germany these stupid idiots are making everything worse day by day. It is up to us to create Wealth, via Crypto and Stocks BUT ALSO other venues. Ths is a Thread for ideas, proven Side-Hustles and more. Let me begin:
- RE-SELLING: I Re-Sell electronics like Consoles, Hi-Fi stuff and sometimes Vintage products. I made good money with PS4s and believe it or not Printers. You get those thrown at you and you can sell it easily for a profit.

- Business Ideas: There are interesting Business Ideas that I am trying to get into which are not Time-Intensive. For example: Washing-Machines for rent! Buy washing-machines, rent them out and make a profit with that. And many more!

What are your Side-Hustles/Business Ideas besides Crypto and Stocks?

Early replies (first 3)

62147137
Shirts

62147140
>>62147136
i want to buy a used CNC machine (vertical mill) for hobby use and also to run jobs to help pay for my costs.

62147168
>>62147137
Seems to be a nice thing - BUT: In which way? Lets say you buy that machine, then you have a couple of options. Do you want to create Logos/Names, do you want people to be able to print their own Logos with your machine?

>>62147140
Had to Google what it is, kek. Thats interesting, so it creates spare parts for machines, did I understand it correctly?

An interesting thought because some Anon posted this in a Thread a while ago: He bought Washing Machines and then stripped it for parts basically. And then sold the parts online on eBay for a profit. Im still thinking of doing this as well.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62147200
>>62147168
>Do you want to create Logos/Names, do you want people to be able to print their own Logos with your machine?
Not just logos but whatever words or things they would want. A lot of the women that come to me with things they want are either just something copied from etsy or just a couple words on a shirt celebrating whatever random event they're doing
This might limit you to simple designs at first but as you get more requests for things you can't do you'll figure out how. For images I can't cut out in vinyl like photos I use a service that prints photos on to vinyl heat transfers
Ideally you can go from businss to business finding out if anybody needs your services, or offer small shops branded apparel they can sell in their stores. A lot of options but you become the salesperson which is where many people, myself included, have a hard time.

62147217
>>62147136
>You get those thrown at you and you can sell it easily for a profit.
Sounds like too much work. How do you source it for a low price (I suppose from some stupid fuckers who don't know how to even google) and for what margin do you sell it? ROI does not make much sense imo and it is hard to scale.
>>62147136
>Washing-Machines for rent! Buy washing-machines, rent them out and make a profit with that
lol lmao even.

I am learning how to use ai and I am trying to sell videos and pics I generate online and make try to weasel my way into social media stuff and selling ai solutions to businesses.
But crypto and stocks will always be king, making my own modell with ai and try to trade with it soon.

62147223
>>62147136
Selling gift cards and accept crypto, like those Bitrefill sites, they make billions from it, there's a big market to be shared.

62147237
>>62147168
>Had to Google what it is, kek. Thats interesting, so it creates spare parts for machines, did I understand it correctly?
yeah basically.

62147255
>>62147217
Its not only Printers of course. What Im doing is basically this: I go on an App where there are listings and categories. I type in Hi-Fi for example, and then Filter the prices. If I see cheap Vintage Sony Speakers, I quickly Re-Search and if they are normally sold for more, I snatch them up. Rinse and repeat with different Inputs (Hi-Fi, Printer, Speakers, PS4, PS5 etc etc etc)
It does work pretty well, but Im not the only one doing it, kek. So I have to be quick and as you said, it is not easy to scale. You are 100% right with that.

The Washing-Machines rent-stuff is pretty interesting IMO - There are some Youtube Videos out there with people doing it and making lots of money with it. BUT: I need place for it which I do not have as of right now.

>>62147223
Can you explain that a little more? Never heard of this.

62147325
>>62147255
>I go on an App where there are listings and categories
You mean vinted or ebay? No way you are making more than 20 % margin with that. Also dead capital if you don't get buyers fast. You can automate that with ai btw so it scans for you based on parameters you decide. Or just write a bot but they often get blocked.

62147350
I was thinking to do door dash on a motorcycle and donate plasma. Between those two things I could make some pretty good extra money for very little effort. But this would just be to supplement my main income, or eventually to support my actual business when I make the leap.

62147354
>>62147325
It really does depend, sometimes I have big margins, sometimes not so much. I did not automate it as of right now. I also frequent Housing Clearances in my region which is really good for nice profits!
But for example I bought a DUAL Turntable from an Old family for 30 Euros. It is worth 250 Euros. I bought a Violine for 20 Euros (Housing Clearance) and its seemingly worth between 300-500 Euros. This of course does not happen every day, but there are pretty good deals out there daily if you look for them.

Mostly its Receivers I buy for between 10-40 Euros and sell them for between 50-99 Euros. I never buy products which are priced correctly if that makes sense.

How would you suggest I automate?

62147359
>>62147350

Plasma is the last easy side hustle that’s why meth heads do it

62147366
>>62147255
>Can you explain that a little more? Never heard of this.

https://www.bitrefill.com/se/en/

62147376
>>62147359
Most door dashers seem like drug addicts too. At least in my town. I like both though because I can make my own hours. No obligations. Haven't done door dash though, not sure if my town is big enough to really support it. We'll see.

62147467
>>62147366
Thank you, Im digging into it right now. How do they make money though? How is this a Billion Dollar Market as you say and how do YOU make money with that?

62147504
>>62147467
I read it in an article a few years back, 1 billion revenue per year, Probably a lot more nowadays.

All you need is to start a website and buy gift card by bulk so you get them cheaper.

If you have these skills then it's like discovering Bitcoin in 2010.

62147541
>>62147137
At least screen print them dude...

62147569
>>62147541
>at least increase your overhead and space requirements by multiple times dude

62147630
>>62147354
Sounds like too much work but I guess if you are short on cash it might help. Just no idea where do you even find open Housing Clearances. I only know about companies who do that and then sell the stuff themselves but that is usually pre picked and you will only find trash there. And something being "worth" vs someone paying it is a huge difference. Something is only worth something if you can find a buyer.
>How would you suggest I automate?
Learn how to write python scrips, you can use ai for that or set up and clawbot or hermes agent with access to your browser. If you make like 30 euro more with it then it pays for itself. Thinking about it maybe I go and set up something like that as a saas to sell to people lmao.
>>62147504
Good idea but you need a lot of upfront capital + trust and a lot of legal bs. I don't think you can make deals where you are just the middle man kind of like dropshiping because then you won't get big disscounts and your margin would be terrible.

62147775
>>62147630
In Germany there is an App, basically like Craigslist and lots of Families put the Housing Clearances on there with Pictures of what is there. I scan the photos, if I see electronics in there then I message and say that I would like to come for the electronics. Often times its just beds, old decorations or "trash" but sometimes there is good stuff to be had for little money.
Thank you for the tips regarding automation! I will look into it.

62147801
>>62147775
What is the app called I also live in pooristan. This shit is so easy to automate, ai has pic recognition so no need to waste your life time. Need some easy cash atm until my other stuff makes me enough.

62147832
>>62147801
The App is called Kleinanzeigen, Im sure you know it if you are in our yearly getting worse Country. Work with the Filters, put the Location on and put the radius to 20km. Search for "Hi-Fi" and put the Filter on cheapest first. Its crazy for how cheap you can get stuff that is much more valuable. Sometimes even for no money at all.
Try it with Electronics (Stereo Anlagen, Drucker, Plattenspieler etc.), and what Im going to start doing is going after Bikes. Good luck!

62147846
>>62147832
>>62147801
Oh and if you want to go after Housing Clearances specially then just type in "Haushalts-Auflösung" in your area. BUT: Lots of people do what Im doing. You have to be quick. I just saw one in my area with 2 great Electronics but I was too late even though it was posted 5 minutes before I messaged.

62148025
>>62147136
Draw futas and claim they're trans women. The less effort you put into your drawings, the better. I've seen that grift generate lots of likes for some reason. People will likely send you money.

item.title: most kiked economy/market of our lifetimes — 15 replies — 50.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
most kiked economy/market of our lifetimes

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-04-23 16:31 UTC

OP

>already-cracking economy/job market
>supposedly no one under 42 has a job
>war happens
>oil shock
>strait of hormuz closes
>somehow this means the stock market reaches new ATH

fuck this tiresome clown show
i just want it to all come crashing down so that whores can't continue even a bare minimum existence and for normie goycattle to be utterly crushed
this retard market is line go up
even the fucking $1 TRILLION debt interest can't slow this shit down
i fucking hate it so much ARGH

Early replies (first 3)

62146925
and i know it's just fucking AI holding it all up
when does this fucking AI bubble pop
i've just had enough

62146994
>>62146925

Spoken like a true traitor. AI industry suffering a downturn would mean that US will loose the AI war against China. No matter what we can't allow that to happen, and will need to divert all public and private investment for construction of AI Data Centers.

62147003
>>62146994
>traitor
that word only holds meaning in the minds of palantir-loving goycattle like you. everyone else is rooting for the total destruction of america as a concept.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62147014
>>62147003
based

62147020
>>62147003
It is simple: we need to direct all the country’s resources towards the Star Gate project for the US to achieve world supremacy in AI and robotics, even if that implies a temporary deterioration of your living standards through inflation and a worsening labour market. Elon Musk already said all US citizens will have Universal High Income when the US wins the AI arms race against China. If you don't agree to those terms, move to Communist China pinko, your kind in not welcomed in America.

62147026
>>62146994
we know agent smith, we know, is the china monster in the room right now?

62147027
>>62147020
silence, jew.

62147039
>>62147003
The Republic of the United States of America died in 1871. Since then we have been living in a Kiked Corporate Territory, not even a “Nation”, just a Corporation. The best thing that could ever happen for humanity would be the destruction of all Corporate Governments. The Banks own and operate them all.

62147044
>>62147039
fully agree. more reason to end america given it acts as a cow that the Corporation leeches off.

62147100
>>62146921
I'm doing fine. Just breached 6 figs a year in salary and I have ~300k in crypto.

62147110
>>62147100
Wow what an accomplishment

62147124
>>62147110
Thanks I'm pretty happy!

62147132
>>62147124
I have reported you to ICE.

62147141
>>62147132
I'm a native American though, as in I was born here to two American parents.

62147982
>>62147141
Okay, Chief Who Buys Up Shitcoins

item.title: What are the chances MSTR goes bankrupt in the next few years? — 12 replies — 48.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
What are the chances MSTR goes bankrupt in the next few years?

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-04-23 16:39 UTC

OP

What are the chances MSTR goes bankrupt in the next few years? Seriously thinking of going all in in a couple of months

Early replies (first 3)

62147678
Do goycattle really just bet on stocks and pray? They can't just make companies rise and fall?

62147679
>>62147669
0 by the looks of btc this week

62147687
>>62147678
We just wait until they fall and sell when they rise. It just takes patience

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62147807
>>62147669
Its earnings depend on Btc going up in price and they have a ton of debt.
What do you think?

62147937
i will buy mstr when bitcoin is around 40K as a leveraged Bitcoin etf

62147944
>>62147937
you think it'll hit 40k? It's almost at 80k as is. Maybe in October? Kind of worried they'll front run it again

62147946
>>62147944
im not worried at all

october is in 6 months

62147959
>>62147946
Do you have a backup plan in case BTC is higher than it is now in 6 months?
If it looks fucked I'm probably just going all in spacex/anthropic/openai IPOs, dca-ing every paycheck and waiting a decade

62147975
>>62147959
i still DCA Bitcoin on a daily basis but the big stack is in USD and EUR

i am not stressed at all, these fake pumps are artificially created in order to make you buy high so the elites buy when its low while you hold bags or (even better for them) you capitulate at the bottom

plebs were telling me that im stupid when i sold in october and november 2026, plebs gonna pleb

62147984
>>62147975
But do you really think it could ever go to 40k again? The last time it was 40k was Jan 2024, which was before the halving. And even after the recent nuke where they tried to push the price to 0, it only dropped to 60k. I think it's actually impossible for it to go below 60k.

62147987
>>62147984
just put the fries in the bag wagie

62148013
>>62147984
This board said same thing back in 2022

item.title: NEED HELP! — 8 replies — 27.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
NEED HELP!

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-04-23 16:31 UTC

OP

Bought 47 used cars, sold them all, put everything into a shitcoin my Discord mod recommended. He's now 'offline' permanently. Can I
write off my losses as 'market research'? Asking for a friend (I am the friend)

Early replies (first 3)

62146656
>>62146432
Buy Foodpepe next time?

62146666
They're not losses unless you sell

62146672
>>62146656
>>62146666
foodpepe is the only shitcoin you can trust

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62146673
Id buy foodpepe next time yes

62146692
>>62146656
>>62146666
>>62146672

62146722
>>62146673
foodpepe makes too much sense

62146738
>>62146432
Don't forget that you can only sell like 5 used vehicles a year if you're not a dealer sol good luck on those taxes too

62147981
>>62146673
Good choice

item.title: You retards don’t think this is a bubble? — 14 replies — 21.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
You retards don’t think this is a bubble?

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-04-23 16:09 UTC

OP

You retards don’t think this is a bubble? You are actually buying SOXL here?

Early replies (first 3)

62140916
i bought fuckin lin (already dumped) bx (already dumped) and just bought brk and hood...soxl lmao?

62140918
>>62140877
the real morons didnt make any money here

62140952
>>62140877
I think understanding reality is that the semiconductor industry and AI are too big to fail. What I mean by that is considering we are dedicating more than 1% of US GDP to this pursuit, we are all fucked if the bubble pops. Like 2008 fucked.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62141008
>>62140877
>>62140952
That sharp dip you're seeing isn't the bubble popping. It'll retrace, then shoot higher. They 'need' their virtual immortality machine to succeed.

62141038
>>62140877
If there's an AI bubble I'll be slurping this and TQQQ so hard

62141045
how many times does it have to go up for it to no longer be a bubble

62141225
>>62140877
This is just normal price recovery due to war price suppression. The industry was doing very well prior to war, dumped massively during war and recovered.

62141230
>>62141045
bears are losers.

62142321
>>62140877
That's just the dollar losing value. Pic related is a bit old but you get the point.

62142346
>>62142321
>in gold terms
so if gold drops 50% redo it (not that gold matters at all)

62142381
>>62142346
If gold drops 50% then you will get another spike in the graph that will eventually go down, and it won't be anywhere near dotcom levels. Pic related is updated to 2026

62145082
all disruptive tech is a possible bubble, there was the dot com bubble but did you stop using the internet when it popped? no

fact is that the current valuations are wayyy too stretched

62146931
it's so fucking niggerish i just can't take it anymore. when does this gayass bubble pop.

62147900
>>62142381
When you think about it, this graph is crazy. All the niggers saying that gold is overvalued should have a look

item.title: Free money glitch IRL — 7 replies — 20.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Free money glitch IRL

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-04-23 16:27 UTC

OP

>make a token with billions of supply
>convince retards and boomers that these tokens will be used for the NWO
>hire social media experts to cater to schizos with money with the bear, and use trading bots to fake volume
>as the token price slowly rises, sell millions of them each month (dump on coin holders)
>use the profits from the tokens you sold to buy businesses
>use said businesses you bought to further gaslight people that you’re slowly “taking over the financial system” e.g a random Japanese bank with 1 single google review, this leads to bag holders being fucked and ripple share holders being rewarded
>unleash the extravod, unbearable bull, trading bots and X shills to go full blast, everywhere on social media’s will now be “XRP is the future and is going to 10k” capture MILLIONS of low IQ copium inhaling bag holders
>price goes even higher
>dump even MORE so that Brad can buy lambos and more tiny businesses, and Joel can buy more cocktails
>US government slowly catches on to this and says XRP is not a commodity to stop this scam
>this somehow makes schizos and bag holders think they’re getting the token at “cheap” and that this is all part of a grand plan
>pay the shills to convince holders that the price tanking is good because “banks are stacking”, and to now cater to a new audience… the religious cultists
>XRP whales and ripple now pay for the religious shills, to say that XRP is the coin from god to commence the wealth transfer, more bag holders acquired
>successfully gaslighted the crypto community that XRP will be the future
>millions of cult like bag holders will now continue to keep buying XRP, allowing ripple to dump the token forever for infinite profits
>free money glitch is OP

When will you realise this biz, XRP is absolutely worthless, ripple has absolutely no use or need for it, only to dump on retail and baggies

Early replies (first 3)

62146454
not reading all of that

62146467
TLDR,

Ripple share holders win, XRP baggies do not… YOU are the exit liquidity for Brad, ripple share holders and for people who got in the grift very early at pennies

62146470
>>62146467
XRP is still up around 3x since the start of the bull market no?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62146484
>>62146470

62146491
>>62146439
>>62146454
>>62146467
>>62146470
>>62146484

TLDR

bullish XRP tho

62146653
>>62146439
BOOOM

62147966
>>62146439
dr;ns
xrp the standard
you are a perennial faggot

item.title: Realvalue assets vs bullshit assets — 6 replies — 14.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Realvalue assets vs bullshit assets

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-04-23 16:24 UTC

OP

Everyone around me is jerking off to index funds and company papers or some "real value" where all he has is some "trust me bro it exists" certificate. Honestly to me its real value assets only, the rest sounds just like a ponzi scam

>>give me your money
>>wait
>>i give you more money (((trust))) me bro

Where is this different to central banking? Bank takes all the assets and you maybe get them if you ask nice but TRUST me bro they all exist despite only a small percentage of them are taken out TRUST me bro we have trillions of goldbars houses and goydollars in store that you cant prove.

All i have is
>BTC with keys on a hardwallet
>Physical gold, silver, trading cards and real estate morgaged on a fixed interrest rate (that i live in)
>Products i can allways use/sell, ammunition, canned food, firewood, car, powertools/tools

I literally dont own a single jewish magic paper certificate that says i own 0,00001% of company cluster nr5

Early replies (first 3)

62147935
based except sell your gay BTC for more physical gold/silver

62147939
>>62147931
real estate is inflated by credit availability. if there were a credit crunch, your plywood box and its land would flash crash, just like it did back in 2008.

62147940
>>62147935
Good luck getting that stuff on an airplane or buying basic goods without exchanging it to goymoney first.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62147947
>>62147940
>muh airplane
i'm not fleeing anywhere, bitch

62147952
>>62147939
But i still would live in it and until i sell it there is no (big) problem. They cant just print houses since the space is finite

62147953
>>62147939
truth
this is when you can buy a house for like 10oz of gold

item.title: My future existence depends on $120 LINK. — 9 replies — 10.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
My future existence depends on $120 LINK.

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-04-23 15:55 UTC

OP

My future existence depends on $120 LINK.

Early replies (first 3)

62141922
kwab

62141936
>>62141921

62141944
>>62141921
same
we will make it brother

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62142856
>>62141921
my future existence depends on $81k LINK

62142880
>>62141921
it might hit 17 next bull run and youll be euephoric,

when you feel so happy and good at 17 sell it all anon

62142918
>>62141921
are you me?

62142922
>>62141921
put that into AMD and actually make it

62147741
It's unironically $1000 for me.

62147827
>op's girlfriend has flat feet
grim

item.title: XRP is the biggest scam ever — 28 replies — 76.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
XRP is the biggest scam ever

Open thread • Replies: 28 • Updated: 2026-04-23 16:26 UTC

OP

they sell token to shift money over to the tradfi company Ripple to buy actual companies and assets and don't pay any dividends or yield on actual xrp tokens. I've seen actual xrp baggies say this is somehow a good thing.

Early replies (first 3)

62143238
Gee, wonder what they could buy

62143321
>>62143203
Means I can buy more for less. BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!

62143438
>>62143203
>ethereum foundation sells ETH and what
>Chainlink labs sells LINK and what

hypocrites and mind raped baggies

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62143537
Biggest and longest running scam in crypto
I sold my ripple shares in 2017 and never looked back

62143560
>>62143438
>>ethereum foundation sells ETH and what
uses the funds to support their protocol
>>Chainlink labs sells LINK and what
uses the funds to support their protocol

>hypocrites and mind raped baggies
the other buys companies and ignores their own blockchain then gas lights their own community and claims that "xrp is the center of everything they do"

62143593
>>62143203
Did they pass the Clarity Act yet? No? When are they gonna pass it? Nobody knows? So, same as yesterday?

62143907
>>62143438
Someone post the alts chipmunk.

62144675
>>62143203
Seething linkbaggie
how is that flippening coming along zach?
https://link-flip.alwaysbeshipp.ing/

62144998
>>62143203
>chainlinkdog obsesses over biggest most obvious scam ever that does the same thing as the company he works for
interesting
would be a real shame if xrp went up in price especially compared to link
I wonder how they will cope then

62145068
>>62143560
so ripple buying prime brokerage and countless other institutional leaning companies to create their ecosystem is just pure dumping on the community? crypto tribal fags are so pathetic

62146230
>>62143438
>>62144998
>>62145068
Ripple dumps on TOKEN HOLDERS to fund things that DO NOT USE THE TOKEN, and explicitly only benefit SHAREHOLDERS.

Nobody else in crypto does this.
How are you people so insanely retarded.

62146371
>>62146230
Chainlink does the exact same thing you dense paid mouthbreather shill
None of the shit that CLL promotes or builds or advertizes has anything to do whit the token itself

62146402
>>62146371
If it uses CL nodes, it uses the token.
So to prove your position, all you have to do is name one CLL product that does not use the nodes.
Go

62146438
>>62146402
name one instance when you had to purchase the token
go

62146458
>>62146371
>it has nothing to do with the token

>>62146438
>ok it does need the token, but you didn't purchase it directly

woosh go the goalposts

62146481
>>62146458
>muh subsidies mean token needed despite the fact that absolutely no one buys it or uses it for anything
you're unironically a moron if you truly believe semantics and nuances are doing anything for you
you've been trying this angle for 6 years to no avail, read the room you spastic

62146485
>>62146371
>it has nothing to do with the token

>>62146438
>ok it does need the token, but you didn't purchase it directly

>>62146481
>ok purchasing directly or indirectly is irrelevant, but some of it is subsidized

Second goalpost move in a row.

62146502
>>62146485
not a single product requires the usage of LINK
no, subsidies do not matter at all, sorry
not a single trial or pilot requires the LINK token at any capacity, not a single institutional partner has ever touched the token
can't wait until you'll finally get doxed and raped in Minecraft

62146506
>>62146502
>not a single product requires the usage of LINK
All products use CL nodes, therefore requiring the usage of Link.

You're probably going to loop right back to "but you didn't purchase the Link directly".

>no, subsidies do not matter at all, sorry
But you're the one who brought up the subsidies.

What a shitty chatbot.

62146532
>>62146506
you do realize that once Chainlink implodes, you along with all the other paid marketeers will be in grave danger? you do realize that attacking your entire online persona around a crypto scamcoin will inevitably end bad for you?
tick, tock...

62146544
>>62146532
>paid marketeer
I'm not the one lying, moving goalposts, forgetting my own talking points, and changing IDs.

Go ahead, name one CL product that does not use CL nodes (and therefore the Link token).

62146565
>>62146506
kill yourself before I find you, Rajesh

62146578
>says some bullshit
>gets debunked
>changes IDs and gets extremely emotional

many such cases, sad!

62147333
>>62145068
>to create their ecosystem is just pure dumping on the community?
What ecosystem? The one they claim is coming? None of the companies they bought have integrated XRPL.

62147341
>>62143203
>dont come to /biz/ for a year
>its still the same fucking threads by cultist linkies and xrp schizos
We’ll see you next year

62147491
>>62147341
it's literally the same 4 retards in every fucking thread

62147758
>>62146458
literally the same can be said of xrp kek

62147964
Chainlink is such a pathetic little project...

item.title: What the best ROI investment I can make on myself? — 85 replies — 60.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
What the best ROI investment I can make on myself?

Open thread • Replies: 85 • Updated: 2026-04-23 15:25 UTC

OP

What the best ROI investment I can make on myself?

Early replies (first 3)

62145347
>>62145342
get a high paying job

62145354
>>62145342
500 test a week

62145355
>>62145347
By just being lucky?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62147005
>>62146978
doctor is fine if it's your passion, but it's boring as hell. and you work hard as a doctor. i literally get to program all day (my hobby) and i work 0-2 hours a week while staying at home in my underwear. there is no other job on earth that is better pay per hour of work (other than CEO) than my job. right now i'm technically "working" btw. i'm on the clock. keep coping, i bet your wife gets 20x more stress than i do. if i wanted to make more money, i can work 3 jobs and eclipse her salary AND have less stress, but the point is to avoid stress. by the way, working at a tier 1 like meta you earn MORE than 500k a year when you consider total comp with my amount of experience, but then you get doctor level stress. cope to death, breh

>>62146983
you are just wrong though. there is no better ROI job currently than software dev. you aren't even a doctor, your wife is (her passion). you're gonna have to take my word for it. it wins on every front, higher income potential through Tier1s, less stress and higher ROI through tier2/3. All you have to do is be able to pass Calc 2 and Linear Algebra, that's a steal

62147006
>>62146996
dental school is challenging but allegedly less difficult than medical school. i probably could have made it through dental school. i hear it's a bad deal these days, though. the costs of education are up by an insane amount and private equity jews (aspen dental etc.) are succeeding in crushing wages.

62147010
>>62147005
notice how dharjeet dodges this post. >>62146976 how many lakhs per annum are you making doing the coding needful for MNC, big sir?

62147011
>>62146989
i didn't misuse since, you just misunderstood the natural break in my sentence due to my lack of punctuation (due to freeform thought-to-text)

"i work 0-2 hours a day, since 3 years ago it's been like this."
i work 0-2 hours a day. in fact, since 3 years ago, it's been like this (for me)

do you understand now, codelet?

62147023
>>62146976
bro what are you even on about? are you having a melty, Pradrash Singh? nice ad hominem since you can't say shit about the goated profession. just pass Calc 2 and you too can be like me. kek, i bet you're a librarian or something

62147025
>>62146992
German doctors with their own practice make 250k+€ a year.
There is no other way in Europe to make that kind of money.

62147031
>>62147025
>>62146992
The best programmer in Europe barely makes 100k€ a year.
The programmers who go to America, like Linux Torvalds, become multi-millionaires.

62147034
>>62147023
>bro
the phrase "since x years ago" has never once entered the mind of a native english speaker. the other tell is your reference to tiers.

62147035
>>62147025
Maybe - i'm not familiar with german wages, but based on misrepresentation of medical salaries here in the UK I'd be sceptical. Common to see news articles here saying "junior doctors striking despite earning £80k!", despite that "junior" being an ST8 ophthalmology reg 12 years out of medical school spending their days doing emergency globe rupture repairs in theatre.

62147038
>>62146976
also most indians/H1Bs are slaves, they don't get to work from home. no one on my team (all fully remote) is Indian, but my last non-remote team had 70% indians (it is what it is). you see them a lot in the early years since a lot of indians do easy/lower-tier programming. that being said there are still great indian programers but they pale in comparisson to codejeets

>>62147034
keep coping, librarian

>the other tell is your reference to tiers
KEK. yeah you'd never make it as a software engineer, you need a relatively high IQ

62147045
>>62147038
your reality is being a brown stinking curry nigger contractor for infosys making $10k USD equivalent per year.

62147049
>>62147035
40 year olds making $107k a year in Britain and then still striking is weird to me. 107k is not bad

62147050
>>62145354
Based

62147051
>>62147045
keep coping, my librarian friend. how does it feel knowing the smelly indians at my last job (non WFH) that were literal slaves that would look down as you walked past them, made more money than you? sad innit? just pass Calculus 2 and be born with a higher IQ and you can make it, subhuman

62147053
>>62147034
I work with all kinds of Europeans and Jeets and yea they think they speak English a lot better than they actually do. We're just very forgiving people.
If Americans treated accents like Europeans do then nobody but Americans would be considered fluent in English - and a few Swedes.

62147057
>>62147035
If you can't figure out how to make a measly 10k a month as a licensed medical doctor you're not trying. And I mean in any country. Even Romania.

62147066
>>62147049
That's an extremely extremely competitive hard speciality and position to get that salary. Plus in medicine you're not controlling for a 40 hour week, you're typically doing 50-60 hours with weekends, evenings and nights. Plus all the crazy expensive and demanding exams on the side that you're practicing for.

I knew an ST2 ophth reg and he had moved to a different UK country for a training post and had seen his 2 month old first born once. It's really not worth the money. I always tell people you could basically just pull double shifts at subway and earn the same as a doc for the same hours with a tenth of the stress.

Then the reality is you aren't an ophth ST8. FY1s earn £26k base (controlled for 40 hours daytime) and it slowly climbs. People then get stuck as locums for years fighting for increasingly rare training posts and have awful job security. I've met lots of people who fly around the country for a week of work here and there.

62147072
>>62147057
By shilling herbal supplements on tiktok like all docs here now seem to do?

It's one of the most embarrassing outcomes of the state of UK medicine, you won't meet a doc who isn't working on some sort of grift on the side and it really cheapens medicine. Everyone is flogging supplements or trying to con the NHS into buying some sort of software.

62147082
>all software developers are Jeets
love this cope. yes there are a fuckton of indians, but i haven't seen them since <5 YOE. they're a terror for new software devs, but they bottom out early. they are hired for the medium-paid grunt/slave work that most software devs start out doing. you literally just stop seeing them after a certain point. ESPECIALLY if you WFH.

and i'm still waiting for someone to tell me a better ROI job. i'm literally working right now doing nothing, sitting at home in front of my monitor with no shirt. what am i gonna do all day? i have a PROD push that i prepped yesterday for, today i'm going to push it, prob in a few hours, then verify that everything is okay in PROD. that's literally it. no actual work, just press a button and read some logs. no meetings except a 5 minute stand-up where i will say "gonna push this to PROD and verify". everyone knows that it's only 30 minutes of work, nobody cares.

name one job that can compete with this. you just need to have probably 115+ IQ and that's it. and even then you could just do front-end or full-stack dev if you have average or even below average IQ, as long as you like to program and put in hours (like doctor subhumans who memorize all day)

62147089
>>62146774
This.
It’s really as simple as mind body and soul. Take care of these three things and the rest will work itself out.

This means exercise and hygiene- body
Education, read and study - mind
Church, prayer, meditation, and devotion-spirit

62147134
>>62146920
ew c section

62147153
>>62145596
never understood why people do not do this. literally grap a chlorox bleach wipe and get down there and clean it. or just run to lowes and grab a replacement. a screen driver and kneeling down for 60 seconds is apparently too much.

62147201
>>62147082
>doesn't understand ROI
>doesn't understand risk
This is why you're low wage and kept out of the board room.

62147210
>>62147066
80k pounds a year is a privilege.
I've heard of doctors going up to 130k a year by working 12 hours per day and just being 35 and not even a consultant.
Doctors are privileged making american salaries sitting in europe

62147219
>>62147066
>I knew an ST2 ophth reg and he had moved to a different UK country for a training post and had seen his 2 month old first born once. It's really not worth the money. I always tell people you could basically just pull double shifts at subway and earn the same as a doc for the same hours with a tenth of the stress.
That's just the beginning, probably the first 2-5 years.
People who graduated from medicine at 24 or something can make a lot by 35.

62147263
>>62145342
A disability

62147280
>>62146796
Based advice

62147356
>>62147201
good one, librarian. your job has so much higher ROI than a goated software engineer working 0-3 hours per week, from home

62147408
>>62147356
I've worked with hundreds of IT and coder fags in my life and not ONCE has any codefag been a guy I could respect or was envious of.
You're probably even a shitlib.

62147696
>>62147408
that's because we're at home, not in an office like some subhuman. someone like me would never run into a sweaty office drone like you. you're the same as the jeet slaves you mock, kek

item.title: >had a russian escort to celebrate reaching 500k in stocks >got aids and gonorrhea FUCK — 43 replies — 47.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>had a russian escort to celebrate reaching 500k in stocks >got aids and gonorrhea FUCK

Open thread • Replies: 43 • Updated: 2026-04-23 15:53 UTC

OP

>had a russian escort to celebrate reaching 500k in stocks
>got aids and gonorrhea
FUCK

Early replies (first 3)

62141740
Deserved

62141751
>>62141715
What was his name?

62141752
>>62141715
that's like fucking a raw chicken and complaining about catching salmonella

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62142929
>>62142149
crazy, didn't know that. same like USA.

Here are the top 25 countries by estimated number of people living with HIV (PLHIV), with adult prevalence (%) for ages 15–49 where available. Figures are rounded estimates from UNAIDS/WHO and other global data sources (most recent published year 2023–2025).

South Africa — ~9,200,000; 17.3%
India — ~2,350,000; 0.2%
Nigeria — ~2,450,000; 1.3%
Mozambique — ~2,480,000; 12.6%
Kenya — ~1,600,000; 4.0%
Uganda — ~1,400,000; 3.0%
Tanzania — ~1,700,000; 3.9%
Zimbabwe — ~2,460,000; 23.1%
Malawi — ~1,640,000; 11.4%
Ethiopia — ~630,000; 0.9%
Zambia — ~1,200,000; 11.0%
Brazil — ~870,000; 0.6%
United States — ~1,200,000; 0.4%
Russia — ~1,200,000; 0.4%
Pakistan — ~210,000; 0.1%
Democratic Republic of the Congo — ~520,000; 0.8%
Indonesia — ~540,000; 0.2%
Cameroon — ~560,000; 3.6%
Afghanistan — ~50,000; 0.1%
Uganda (if duplication) — note: regional lists vary; see notes below.
Sudan — ~210,000; 0.4%
Vietnam — ~250,000; 0.3%
Argentina — ~140,000; 0.4%
Côte d’Ivoire — ~360,000; 2.9%
Ghana — ~330,000; 1.7%

62142941
>>62142929
Still the same as Argentina and lower than Brazil

62145135
>>62142929
Honestly surprised Japan isn't up there.

62145137
>>62141715
>has over 6 figures
>fell for the hookers meme
what the fuck is this world

62145138
>>62141715
I've been with over 30 prostitutes and never got anything. I've never done anything raw besides for once when I was like 22

62145141
>>62142929
>africa
fucking disgusting. and these people are flooding into my country

62146044
>>62142149
European market is currently full of Russian escorts. Watch Europe STD rates soar

62146056
>>62145135
In developed nations it's predominantly intravenous drug users followed by homosexuals (the chance is higher for transmission with unprotected anal sex, but PReP drugs and condoms are now more commonly used by homosexuals).

62146064
>>62141715
black people also all have aids. they're the same.

62146119
>>62145138
ive slept with anywhere 500-1000 women, all raw and most times i ate their pussy

and the only thing i got was an hpv flare up on my cock. from a girl from israel that was workng at a kiosk in the mall it was just a little wart and hasnt come back in decades

62146147
wow is that training for spetznatz? The ukrains are fucked!

62146161
>>62141715
>aids
So you let some russian dude fuck you in the ass? Because its almost impossible to get aids from straight sex so long as you don't have open wounds or some other form of blood to blood contact.

62146191
Getting covid is far safer than this toy virus

62146195
>>62142929
>United States — ~1,200,000; 0.4%
>Russia — ~1,200,000; 0.4%

How can they be the same percentage when the USA has multiple times as many people?

This is sort of beside the point too. The Russian rate isn't super high, although it is huge compared to the rest of Europe, it's that they have long been 3-9% of ALL global new infections, despite being a tiny sliver of the population. Part of that is better reporting but a big part is prostitution, IV drug use, and endemic rape, including gay rape in the military and conscripts used as prostitutes.

62146225
>>62141715
>Trusting or supporting Russians at all

62146357
>>62146225
>west doesn’t understand the link between Ukraine and Russia
>they will lay their arms and join their Russian brother
In reality
>fucking fuck, here is a drone in your ass

62146360
>>62141715
Next time don't let Russian trannies pop your bootyhole without a condom. You're not Iron Man.

62146893
>>62146225
that tweet at 43 seconds, this is what they're saying now
https://caityjohnstone.medium.com/some-thoughts-on-ukraine-eefabafb6999

What is wrong with these people?

62147144
>>62146893
From that article
>Now, some may hear this and say “Okay but Russia still shouldn’t have invaded even though our western leaders were aggressively provoking them to.” But before you do that it might be a good idea to look inside yourself and ask where that impulse is arising from. Why are you so eager to skip past the part where you criticize your own rulers for their role in starting this war and focus solely on criticizing the leader of an eastern government who has no power over you? What is it inside of you that’s flailing all over the place trying to avoid any forceful scrutiny of the reckless warmongering of your own government and its allies?
Yeah, seriously, why are they flail around so much to avoid criticizing warmongering Russia? lol
But seriously, it’s crazy how Ukraine pretty much beated russia

62147295
>>62147144
Do you people actually really believe this? Or is it more of a mine team is winning thing? Because every serious situation report I read is that Ukraine is getting creamed from the Americans to the chinese

62147314
>>62145138
Licked a whore's butthole once. She tongued mine without asking and I thought I'd be nice and return the favor. I thought if I ever caught anything from a pro, that would have done it but so far so good, knock on wood.
Also, paying for pussy at a set rate is a terrible way to waste money. Almost as bad as divorce

62147514
>>62147295
Dude, I know, two more weeks in Kiev.
Seriously man, let it go. Russia lost

62147606
Good Lord you people are vile and disgusting. You make me physically sick.

62147624
>>62147606
say something of substance or just kill yourself/fuck off to r*ddit. Nobody cares about your tone policing and faux-outrage Karen.

62147735
>>62147514
Oke so it is a mine team is winning thing instead of reality sad many such cases

62147751
>>62142929
>23% Zimbabwe

Absolute insanity. Why are blacks so susceptible to HIV?

62147760
>>62145138
Just so you know most STDs show zero symptoms so you could actually have everything and not know. Only way to know for sure is get tested

62147780
>>62147735
Anon stop it. Lots of pro Russia guys keep posting gore about how Ukraine are being massacred and soon we take all their women.
Literal genocidal stuff in a light tone.
But now that the table are turned it’s “my team, your team”?

62147817
>>62147751
Low impulse control
Below average intelligence and therefore low genuine risk assessment
>>62147780
You're the one turning it into a team sport, tiger
Its a meat grinder attrition war and ukr has lost land that it will not be getting back
As far as russias strategic goals? Getting access to the black sea for better trade routes. They're on track to get there (far slower than they wanted to I'm sure)
Ukraines strategic goals seem to be to kill as many .mil aged men as possible and launder as much aid money as they imagine they can get away with before the music stops.
Both sides are simultaneously winning and losing big, unless you're thinking of the rus/ukr citizenry. They're both just losing big

62147823
>>62147780
My team is my portfolio and reality is good for my team so I know your full of shit you seem emotionally invested in this for some reason not my problem

item.title: Do you flex your BTC to your family or nah? — 33 replies — 22.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Do you flex your BTC to your family or nah?

Open thread • Replies: 33 • Updated: 2026-04-23 15:19 UTC

OP

Wondering if people actually talk about their BTC with family or just keep it lowkey.

>We never really know how long we have in life, so is it better to share it or keep it private?

Early replies (first 3)

62138671
>>62138453
I only have 1 so that's nothing to flex

62138680
People with more money than you won't give a shit and people with less money than you will resent you.

Keep your mouth shut and if anyone questions your purchases or your lifestyle, pretend to be in debt.

62138700
>>62138453
Keep that shit private, openly talking about how much money you have casually is trashy. Only discuss money with family with the intention of planning / sharing, otherwise they'll always have the thought that you have more to yourself. Speaking from experience with a good family, money brings out the absolute worst in people.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62139442
>>62138671
Okay, but what if something bad happens to you? You still won’t be able to pass it on even if it’s 1 BTC, even though its value could be much higher in the future.

62139556
>>62139442
>You still won’t be able to pass it on even if it’s 1 BTC,
What are you talking about kek. You sound confused

62139618
>>62138453
i make mom clean my room for 5 satoshis

62140241
>>62138680
Okay, I understand what you're saying about.

62141418
>>62138700
You also have a point. Do you keep it for your soon to be senpai or when you're married?

62141768
>>62138700
Bad decision

62141874
>>62139618
why so poor?

62142197
>>62138671
I can't believe people on this board are shrimps

62142226
Im still in my 40s and Ive got 63 btc. I'm taking care of my health and aiming until 100 years old. I will enjoy my money for the rest of my life.

62142464
I don't tell anyone how much crypto i have, if my gf asks where I got so much money from I tell her it was from saving it, not going to target to drop $200 because I'm bored.

62142867
>>62139556
I’m not confused. I’m just saying it would be a waste if he couldn’t pass it on in case something happened to him, and his BTC would just end up stuck there.

62142904
>>62138453
I haven’t even told my imaginary friend. Been holding for over half a decade now.

62143627
>>62139618
send proof or else.

62144918
>>62142197
It's sad

62145039
>>62138453
story behind the pic?

62145144
>>62145039
There was a thread here about what you spend your gains on and someone spelt a lot on a horsehide leather jacket. People kept telling him to post the jacket.

62145154
I just lie about my personal wealth. Everyone thinks I'm poor and buys me things. If they thought I had money, they'd beg me for money. Fuck that. Plus they'd probably talk about me to bad people and I'd get bad people after me.

Stealth wealth is the way to go.

62145244
>>62139442
>>62142867
Well you tell your fucking wife or put it in your will where to find the 5 parts to your wallet (of which they need 2)

62145795
>>62141768
What do you mean?

62146229
>>62142904
I hold bitcoin

62146231
>>62145154
Stay lowkey

62146326
>>62146229
You can also stake your BTC if you want to earn from it, so it’s not just sitting idle without generating any yield.

62146497
>>62146326
Is it even safe to do that

62146523
>>62145244
Lmao

62146529
>>62146497
Defi protocols this year are brutal so why still choose this shit

62146577
>>62145154
People will expect you to be Santa everyday

62146661
>>62145144
Is it expensive?

62146671
>>62146529
Better safe than sorry goys

62147207
>>62146497
Yeah it’s safe since you stay in self custody while staking BTC for yield. Look into Babylon if you want to see how it actually works.

62147680
>>62146661
I think the jacket was $1000 lel

item.title: >nofap >religion >stock tips >meditation >therapy >healing crystals >self-help books >pomodoro >PDFs >affirmations >posi… — 40 replies — 18.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
>nofap >religion >stock tips >meditation >therapy >healing crystals >self-help books >pomodoro >PDFs >affirmations >posi…

Open thread • Replies: 40 • Updated: 2026-04-23 14:36 UTC

OP

>nofap
>religion
>stock tips
>meditation
>therapy
>healing crystals
>self-help books
>pomodoro
>PDFs
>affirmations
>positive thinking
>DCA
>crypto
>"self improvement"

Early replies (first 3)

62143060
>/biz/: Business & Finance

62143073
Different ways of coping. No boomber ever had to read the meditations or go to the gym

62143145
>>62143073
the boomers who did DCA have generational wealth now

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62144381
>>62144238
That's frankly a pretty retarded way of looking at it
Women have been unfaithful since the dawn of time and the only thing you can blame aurelius for was trusting somebody with bad faith actions. Getting assblasted because of every little thing is peak niggerdom and if rome acted like that (there are periods they did), the world would have been much worse off for it. Don't be le stoic and force yourself to passively take every loss, failure, and slight like a doormat; just don't chimp out at every opportunity because you think you can

62144395
>>62143054
All you need is Catholicism and the rest will work itself out.

62144435
>>62144381
>Women have been unfaithful since the dawn of time
>trusted a woman
Why he trusted a woman if you say women were always unfaithful dumbass?
Your statement is very incosistent, he is not to blame for trusting a woman yet you say trusting a woman is retarded. So why you defend him. Dumb. I really don't get the Marcus Aurelius dickride

62144457
>>62144435
That's not even what I said, but I'm not surprised you can't see past an argument and a mindset somebody else gave you. 'Women have been unfaithful for forever' is not the same as 'every woman is going to be unfaithful for forever'
I also dont follow stoicism, that macro's reasoning is retarded is my whole point. Defending against bad arguments is also not the same as 'dickriding,' but being brown will put you at a disadvantage for understanding that as well
>inb4 n-not brown! n-no u!
Then stop thinking like a nigger

62144475
>>62143054
>self affirmations are things people that made it do
I actually say
>i am top 10%
>i made it
Multiple times a day

62144478
>>62144457
>Women have been unfaithful for forever' is not the same as 'every woman is going to be unfaithful for forever
Good thing nobody wants advice about women from retards with such retarded statements

62144497
fcuk you OP I scrolled down and was accidentally breathing in when I saw that image and now I feel weird.

62144501
>>62144289
Only people who believe that their ancestors were shat out of a monkeys ass believe there is no meaning to this life.

62144519
>>62144501
Lol ok kid let's see if you gonna say that on your deathbed or if you get cancer. Retarded NPCs like you will never be saved

62144572
>>62144475
>top 10%
>made it

kek

62144576
>>62143145
a monk would live like this but without the faggot TV and gaming setup
that's what bothers me about these "Let me guess. You need more?" images, they're not based and ascetic if you have a faggot TV

62144982
>>62144519
Wtf is that even supposed to mean?

62144995
>>62144982
There's a few active anons on the board that just try to be as caustic and derisive towards any topic or poster as possible. There's at least 2 itt

62144996
>>62144982
It means you are a retarded brownoid faggot basedboy cuck. Kill yourself

62145001
>>62144995
>>62144996
Boom right on cue
They are generally pretty quick to reply as well because that kind of derangement generally stems from being terminally online

62145005
I've read
>how to win friends and influence people
>art of war
>12 rules by peterson
>48 laws of power
>atomic habits
>the art of not giving a fuck
>the art of the deal
>meditations of auruelius(stoic)
>4 hour work week
>the secret
>rich dad poor dad and the sequel

And yet my life still sucks. And before you say "but anon your'e supposed to do the things the books say to do not just read them!" well I have. For example richdad poordad says to borrow money from your friends and buy a house, flip it and then give them back their money-the exact amount they let you borrow. Well I asked all my friends for money and NONE of them gave me any. Only one even asked what it was for and when I told him he laughed in my face and said "You have to know how to flip a house to flip a house."

What the fuck

62145007
>>62145001
>>62144995
>generally pretty quick to reply
The irony. Also you have to be 18+ to post here

62145009
>>62144501
>the meaning to this life is uhhhh if you're a good boy and follow this book, you'll have an infinitely long life after this one, 100% guaranteed, and in that life everything will be great, okay?

62145415
>>62143054
>>nofap
Difficult to do, confers immediate health and psychological benefits, and every normie has a bizarre level hatred for it.

That should tell you something.

62145428
>>62144238
Of course stoicism is a cuck mentality. It's a philosophy of decadence. No Greek during the peak of their civilization would have created something like stoicism the same way no boomber would have been interested in the meditations by marcus aurelius.

When the real world becomes too painful, many people retire and create ways of coping.

62145464
>>62145428
Real stoicism is a an "emptying"/devotion of yourself to higher Being. This is a living, communicative path that taps you into energetic circuits and correspondence. Stoicism as most people know it isn't transformative in any higher sense.

62145495
>>62145464
Well, I meant decadence in the societal sense. Individuals live in a society. Stoicism was born exactly when the greek dream ended and that's why it became so popular. Same way as Epicureism.

And that's why it is so goddamn popular now and you have depressed 21 yo kids talking about stoicism. It has become a meme. You're depressed? Read The Meditations and go to the gym, bro. The real world has become painful and people have to find ways to deal with the real world.

ps. By the way, Seneca moral writings are way more interesting than Marcus Aurelius' Meditations

62145579
>>62145009
Correct
>t. full trust that I'm where I need to be when I need to be there

62145590
>>62145579
retardmaxxing with extra steps

62145626
>>62145590
I wish I could impart to you even 1% of the things I've seen, alas just know its sort of like a Chinese finger trap you know what I mean?
>retardmaxxing
Okay, okay! Based!

62145653
>>62143054
i went all in on long distance ejaculation training, sucks to suck, peace i gotta catch a flight, there are some ceilings that need coating

62145752
>>62145005
rich dad poor dad taught me to put all my money in pokemon cards

62147432

Abandon ship

62147439
It costs zero dollars to not be so darned rude

62147451
>>62143054

fuck i forgot about pomodoro. imagine having a faggy little tomato timer on your desk as an adult

item.title: Gambling with 100 dollars — 24 replies — 17.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Gambling with 100 dollars

Open thread • Replies: 24 • Updated: 2026-04-23 15:28 UTC

OP

What is a good beginner amount of money to start fucking around with 0dte contracts? I was thinking I could try gambling 100 dollars split between several SPY .10 to .20 0DTE contracts throughout the week. What is the best source to learn more about this stuff? What tips can you give from personal experience?

62146209
@anabeloha

62146271

Body 9

62146350

62146367
>>62146258

HALT EVILDOER!

>>62146281
quick, m’lady, make haste into thy chariot and I shall escort you to safety!

62146462
>>62146184
My dick doesn't work like it used to. I'm only 27. If I force the issue I can ejaculate but man does it take some work. At 17, I'd have never believed I'd end up in this state.

62146546
>I remember
>when I was told of story of
Crushed velvet, candle wax, and dried up flowers
>The figure on the bed all dressed up in roses, calling
>Beckoning to sleep, offering a dream
>words were as mystical as purring animals
>the circle of rage, the ghosts on the stage appeared
>The time was so tangible, I'll never let it go
>ghost stories handed down reached >secret tunnels below
>No one could see me
>I fell into yesterday
>Our dreams seemed not far away
>I want to, I want to, I want to stay
>I fell into fantasy

62146556
>>62146462
Stop beating your meat

62146567
>>62146224

>think of child
Get help, pedo

62146948
>>62146184

62147283
>>62146220

62147332

62147361
>>62146224

Not just the women, a lot of men also age much slower. But if you look at her body shape, she is clearly an adult

62147400

62147422

62147700

Nothing to do with porn or jerking off, it's just a low libido and sex anxiety. I jerkoff to porn daily and I still fuck my gf daily as well and I'm 32.

item.title: Polkadot - Mumbai Warrior Edition — 81 replies — 9.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - Mumbai Warrior Edition

Open thread • Replies: 81 • Updated: 2026-04-23 07:52 UTC

OP

Hello my fellow PolkaCurry brothers. We are again here, for another thread where we discuss everything related to DOT, Mumbai (the city of DOT), curry and more. The last thread was a resounding success, we managed to keep it alive for literally 41 days. We got paid 11500 + 5*150 = 12250 DOT for the thread and are now living in a period of enlightenment while eating with METAL spoons. Brothers, we have truly reached enlightenment. WE, the Mumbai Warriors will strike yet again and collect generations worth of money !

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

Early replies (first 3)

62115437
We are eating with our METAL spoons in Mumbai. No more hands my brethren. We eat with out spoons, like proud warriors.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62115624
>>62115424
>>62115437

Still gonna larp as an Indian?

62115793
>>62115437

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62122759
>>62121922
>>62121953

Chart looks good

But in the last thread weren't you shorting DOT

62123242
>>62122759
Panjali, be quite you fool (https://boards.4chan.org/biz/thread/61932834#p62069282). Do you want the Mumbai warriors to eat with their hands again? Or do you want to upgrade from our metal spoons to silver and then golden spoons? We, the Mumbai warriors, are painting the graph bullish in order to lure people in, then we strike with a x125 short. We actually been holding short open since 55$. The whole of Mumbai will eat from that singular short. Panjali, you have much to learn about the Vishnu ways. It's ok my son. I forgive you. Together we are strong, divided we are weak.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62124594

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62126677

Dot 1,15 x37= 42

62126931

62126975

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62127244
>>62126677
Vrishank, that is really good mathematics. Education is really paying off I see. Very good. You might even become a doctor or a lawyer. Yes, you shall reach to the heavens of nirvana one day my young Vrishank. I, Vishnu Kumar, believe in you and so should you my young Vishnu warrior of the mind chain, my young Mumbai warrior, my young metal spoon owner. Soon, we will have silver spoons for everyone, then gold spoon and after that we reach the nirvana of diamond spoons. The whole of Mumbai will eat.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62127578

62127673
>>62127578

62128205
>>62126931

62128210
>>62128205

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62129040
>>62128205

62129456
>>62129040

62129518
>>62129040
>>62129456
No, it's actually Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62131415
100$ end of bull run


Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62131658
It is still cheap right now. Don't buy my bags at 25$. You can buy them now at 1.28$. Don't FOMO in at 50$ guys.

62131731
>>62116681
>What exactly does it have that sets it apart from Solona and ETH?
Solana has a success rate associated with it's transactions (see https://solscan.io/analytics). That's kind of fucked up that there's a 10 to 20% chance your tx might not get processed, right? I've never seen a DOT tx fail. DOT doesn't even measure failed tx https://data.parity.io/parachain-stats?selection-mode=include&frequency=daily&relay-chain=polkadot&chains= because there are none. If you submit a tx it'll complete within like 6s to 30s. The longest a tx takes is when you transfer from one corechain to another. So when you bridge (use a HRMP channel) between corechains. And even that never took longer than like 40s from my personal experience. It's bizarre to me that literally up to 20% of the tx simply fail.
Another thing is that SOL literally has more "vote" transactions than anything else; https://solscan.io/analytics#txs . And vote transactions are LITERALLY validator messages to keep the network running. DOT doesn't count that as a tx at all. 60 to 80% of the tx on SOL are from validators to keep the network running. That's insane to think about that you need more tx to run the network than the users use to execute stuff. People are stupid for thinking that SOL processes 3k TPS. More than half of that is just to keep the network running. It's a literal scam number to be honest. It's just to make the normies believe that the network isn't a scam. The number of transactions is completely fake in a sense.
And again, the funny thing is that 1 core of DOT will be able to handle more TPS than the whole of SOL once either JAM or NOMT hit. Maybe even before that once the 500ms Basti blocks hit. If you combine 500ms blocks + JAM + NOMT one core of DOT will be able to probably do anywhere between 2 to 5x more than SOL as a whole and DOT has LITERALLY 100 cores and this will also probably be scaled to more sooner or later. It'll sooner or later scale to about 300 to 500 cores.

62131777
>>62116681
>>62131731
The 300-500 cores are after the network upgrades to 1000 validators from 600 and after a few more future optimizations. But DOT can then scale further by adding more relay chains. So, currently there is 1 relay chain that connects all the corechains (while they also have direct connections through the HRMP channels) and this relay chain has 600 active validators at any given time. At some point DOT could scale further by having multiple relay chains and separate validators on each relay chain and then having a "relay chain" that connects the relay chains. Sure, this would fragment liquidity in a sense but you could still transfer assets between the relay chains in probably like 30s or so... So in a sense the fragmented liquidity would be a non-problem. DOT is truly scalable while other chains simply are not. DOT covered all the problems because Gavin used to develop ETH and noticed "OMG, this won't work out" and then he left ETH in order to develop DOT. DOT is ETH done right in a sense. ETH is just BTC with smart contracts and DOT is an actual solution to all the problems that plague ETH and all the other smart contract platforms.

62135297
100$ end of bull run

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62137662
Soon we shall reach the nirvana of 75$ my Mumbai brothers. Then we shall eat with silver spoons.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62139771
You are not the body, you are not the mind, you are not your emotions. You are the silent DOT in the infinity of consciousness. You are just a wave in the ocean of consciousness that forgot it is part of the ocean. For when rememberance dawns, the mystery is not solved. It is lived.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62141372
My brethren, the time is nigh. We're break out. The wave of consciousness is breaking free from the ocean floor.

62143550
Any second now BTC will erupt into a storm that has never been seen before. DOT will soon start it's ascension to the heavens for 75$+. We, the Mumbai warriors, shall eat with silver spoons very soon. The time is coming my warriors. You still have to to buy a big fat bag of DOT before you get priced out.

62143825
>>62129518

>>62143550

This is sad. Also stop larping as an Indian and perhaps you'll get engagement

62144010
>>62143825
>This is sad.
My brother, the sadness is in your own heart. Not mine. You are destroying yourself.

>Also stop larping as an Indian and perhaps you'll get engagement
My brother, I am the one and only Vishnu Kumar. Born and raised in India. I am brown as the mud itself. There is no denying it. I am a true Mumbai warrior. I am a proud Indian.
Also Panjali, didn't people teach you that questions are fundamental for a healthy conversation. My young Panjali, I am not here for engagement. If you asked I would've told you so. Oh young Panjali. You still have a long way ahead of you. If accept, I let go, I move on and I forgive you young Panjali.

Thank you for you kind bump of the thread Panjali. I shall later come to your house and give you your share of the profits.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62146419
My fellow Mumbai warriors. We are all already enlightened, we just need to uncover it. DOT is already the best chain, people just need to realize it. They're still looking at the shadow of their light and misidentifying it for themselves. Soon they shall become aware of it and pivot towards the light.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors
